reasoning: CL. Mature year (120 months); actual is $45.0M, nearly at tail-adjusted ultimate. All methods converge tightly ($46.4M-$46.9M). CL is most credible for mature tail development. IE and BF provide anchoring and stability checks.

CL (lightly). Mature year (108 months); actual is $59.9M. CL ($63.0M) is slightly higher than IE/BF ($63.5M-$63.0M), all methods converge. Maturity and convergence support CL dominance; modest IE/BF weight for trend assessment.

CL/BF (blended). Development age 96 months; actual is $35.4M. CL is $38.4M, IE is $59.5M (notably higher), BF is $40.1M. CL-BF consensus ($38.4M-$40.1M) more credible than IE outlier. Even blending avoids anchoring to IE without cause.

CL-favored blend. 84 months development; actual is $55.3M. CL ($62.7M) and BF ($61.7M) align closely; IE also $54.3M. Methods coherent. CL gets slight edge for reliable tail development at this maturity; BF weight increased to check stability.

CL-BF blend. 72 months development. CL ($48.4M), IE ($52.6M), BF ($49.1M). CL and BF align closely; IE slightly higher. At this point in development, CL LDF assumptions still strong but BF exposure logic gains credibility. Equal weight to both.

BF-favored blend. 60 months development. CL ($44.96M), IE ($48.4M), BF ($45.7M). CL-BF are close; IE higher. As development depletes CL credibility, BF (which re-weights earned premium) becomes more important. BF-CL consensus with BF leading.

BF-dominant. 48 months development. CL is $79.1M (highest), IE $46.2M, BF $69.2M. CL appears to be in a tail-driven spike; BF-IE more moderate. At this development stage, BF's earned-premium re-weighting is most credible. Selection anchors to BF.

BF-dominant. 36 months development. CL ($46.5M), IE ($52.4M), BF ($48.9M). BF and CL align; IE higher. At 36 months, BF's explicit exposure and premium adjustment is most defensible for young years. CL downweighted; IE downweighted for outlier tendency.

BF-favored. 24 months development. CL ($48.9M), IE ($47.4M), BF ($48.1M). Methods converge, but at 24 months CL has minimal observed development; BF's earned-premium re-weighting more appropriate. Balanced IE-BF with BF leading.

BF-dominant with IE check. Only 12 months development. CL ($66.3M) appears inflated by tail assumption (1.0309) on minimal run-off. IE ($36.6M) is conservative; BF ($43.8M) is middle ground. At 12 months, CL tail-factor dependency is highest. BF's earned premium and development factor logic is most credible; IE serves as floor check.

CL-favored. Mature year (120 months). CL ($53.1M) higher than IE/BF ($63.9M-$54.7M), driven by the 1.1787 tail factor. Paid development is slower than incurred, making CL tail assumption appropriate here. CL gets edge; BF check included.

CL-dominant. 108 months development. CL ($72.9M) notably higher than IE ($63.5M) and BF ($71.1M), clearly driven by paid development tail. CL is appropriate for paid development at this maturity. IE downweighted for being outlier.

CL-BF blend. 96 months development. CL ($45.4M), IE ($59.5M), BF ($48.7M). CL and BF align; IE is outlier. Paid development slower than incurred; CL/BF consensus more credible. Balanced CL-BF with BF upweighted vs incurred due to IE divergence.

CL-favored. 84 months development. CL ($72.3M), IE ($54.3M), BF ($67.4M). CL significantly higher, reflecting paid development tail. At 84 months, paid tail assumptions credible. CL gets edge with BF stabilization.

CL-BF blend. 72 months development. CL ($56.8M), IE ($52.6M), BF ($55.4M). CL highest but methods align reasonably. Paid tail less mature than 84-month development; CL-BF consensus more defensible than CL alone.

CL-BF equal weight. 60 months development. CL ($51.1M), IE ($48.4M), BF ($50.0M). Methods align well; CL-BF are nearly identical. At 60 months, paid development is material but not fully mature; equal CL-BF weight appropriate, with IE downweighted slightly.

BF-dominant. 48 months development. CL ($88.1M) is a significant outlier; IE ($46.2M), BF ($68.2M). CL tail assumption (1.1787) likely too aggressive on 48-month data. BF's earned-premium and exposure logic is most credible. BF dominates; IE used as downside check.

BF-favored. 36 months development. CL ($53.4M), IE ($52.4M), BF ($52.8M). Methods converge, but at 36 months CL tail assumption carries less evidence. BF's earned-premium logic more appropriate. BF leads with CL-IE as checks.

BF-favored with IE-CL checks. 24 months development. CL ($56.6M), IE ($47.4M), BF ($50.3M). CL higher due to tail factor; IE conservative. At 24 months with minimal paid development, BF's approach most credible. BF leads, balanced IE-CL for range.

BF-dominant with IE check. Only 12 months paid development. CL ($75.7M) heavily tail-driven (1.1787 on 12 months is most speculative); IE ($36.6M) is conservative floor; BF ($43.5M) is balanced. BF's explicit paid development and exposure adjustment is most defensible. IE serves as downside anchor.

CL. Mature year (120 months); actual is 4,930 claims, ~99.8% of all methods. CL ($4,959.7), IE ($6,554.4), BF ($4,969.2). CL and BF converge; IE is notably higher (exposure-driven). CL credible for mature development.
CL. 108 months development. CL ($4,972.8), IE ($6,320.8), BF ($4,980.8). CL and BF align tightly; IE is significant outlier. CL-BF consensus high maturity. CL gets edge.
CL-favored. 96 months development. CL ($4,941.2), IE ($5,759.1), BF ($4,954.0). CL and BF converge; IE higher. CL slightly favored with BF check.
CL-favored. 84 months development. CL ($5,015.7), IE ($5,102.5), BF ($5,017.9). All methods converge very tightly. CL slightly favored for reliable tail; BF provides check.
CL-BF balanced. 72 months development. CL ($5,001.2), IE ($4,796.8), BF ($4,991.6). CL and BF align; IE is lower. Methods converge reasonably. Balanced CL-BF with IE weight for boundary.

CL-BF blended. 60 months development. CL ($5,027.8), IE ($4,287.2), BF ($4,964.4). CL slightly higher; IE lower. CL-BF close. At 60 months, methods are credible but no single method stands out; balanced weighting.

Balanced blend. 48 months development. CL ($5,164.6), IE ($3,973.7), BF ($4,965.5). CL higher; IE lower; BF middle. At 48 months, CL tail assumptions less certain but visible development supports growth. Balanced approach with CL slight edge.

CL-BF balanced with IE check. 36 months development. CL ($5,081.8), IE ($4,373.4), BF ($4,870.8). CL higher; IE lower; BF mid-range. At 36 months, early development visible but tail assumptions speculative. Balanced CL-BF.

IE-BF favored. 24 months development. CL ($4,915.4), IE ($3,842.3), BF ($4,424.1). CL higher and most tail-driven. At 24 months, minimal reported development observed. IE-BF equal weight more credible than CL tail assumption.

IE-BF dominant. Only 12 months development. CL ($5,309.9) is highly tail-driven on minimal data; IE ($2,880.1), BF ($3,663.5). CL tail assumption (1.0060) applied to 1,712 actual is speculative. IE and BF's explicit methods more credible. IE weight highest as conservative.

CL-favored. Mature year (120 months); actual is 4,931 claims. CL ($4,983.8), IE ($6,554.4), BF ($5,000.5). CL and BF converge tightly; IE high. CL-BF consensus is credible. CL gets slight edge for mature tail development.
CL-favored. 108 months development. CL ($4,988.8), IE ($6,320.8), BF ($5,015.7). CL and BF converge; IE outlier. Methods tightly convergent; CL slightly favored with BF check.
CL-favored. 96 months development. CL ($4,948.4), IE ($5,759.1), BF ($4,988.4). CL and BF converge; IE outlier. CL slightly favored; BF provides check.

CL-favored. 84 months development. CL ($5,074.4), IE ($5,102.5), BF ($5,076.7). All methods converge tightly. CL and BF nearly identical; IE marginally higher. CL slightly favored; methods all credible.
CL-BF balanced. 72 months development. CL ($5,011.6), IE ($4,796.8), BF ($4,983.4). All converge reasonably. CL slightly higher; balanced weighting appropriate.
CL-BF balanced. 60 months development. CL ($5,021.4), IE ($4,287.2), BF ($4,874.7). CL and BF align; IE lower. At 60 months, CL-BF credible; balanced weighting with slight BF upweight.

CL-favored blend. 48 months development. CL ($5,398.1), IE ($3,973.7), BF ($4,979.0). CL notably higher; IE significantly lower; BF mid-range. CL visible development growth supports higher selection. Balanced with slight CL edge.

CL-favored blend. 36 months development. CL ($5,279.1), IE ($4,373.4), BF ($4,908.3). CL notably higher; development visible. At 36 months, observable closure development supports CL growth. Balanced with CL edge.

IE-CL balanced. 24 months development. CL ($4,852.8), IE ($3,842.3), BF ($4,303.3). CL higher and most tail-driven; IE conservative; BF moderate. At 24 months, minimal closure development observed; IE-CL balanced with IE upweighted for early-maturity conservatism.

IE-BF dominant. Only 12 months closure development. CL ($4,649.7) is tail-driven (1.0107 on 1,306 actual is speculative); IE ($2,880.1), BF ($3,377.2). At 12 months, CL tail assumption highly uncertain. IE conservative logic and BF development approach more credible. IE weight highest.
